questions: Members table name: `brain-of-bndc/src/common/storage_handler.py:190-209` writes to `members`, not `discord_members`. The brief repeatedly says `discord_members`. Should all new FK references target `members(member_id)` (my assumption), or does the website already use a `discord_members` view/table I can't see from this sandbox?

Attachment ID source: the archive stores only `{url, filename}` per attachment (no Discord `attachment.id`). Is it acceptable to derive `discord_attachment_id` by parsing the `/attachments/{channel_id}/{attachment_id}/{filename}` segment of the CDN URL (stable across URL-signature refreshes), with `(discord_message_id, filename)` as a secondary dedup if parsing fails?

OP attachment classification: the brief adds `'discord-comment'` to `media.classification` for reply attachments but says 'decide convention in plan' for OP attachments. What value should OP imports use — an existing value already present in the `media.classification` enum (e.g. `'post'` / `'primary'` / whatever manual uploads use), or a new `'discord-post'` value?

Asset column shape: the brief writes `member_id`, `name`, `description`, `type`, `admin_status` into `assets`. Is `admin_status='Listed'` a valid value in the existing CHECK/enum, and is `type` a free-text column or an enum constrained to `('lora','tool', ...)`?

pg_cron secret pattern: does the shared Supabase project already use `vault.secrets` + `app.settings.service_role_key` for authenticated `net.http_post` from cron (my assumption), or is there a different pattern I should match?

Markdown rendering on the website: does `banodoco-website` already depend on a markdown renderer I should reuse for comment `content`, or do I need to add `react-markdown`? (Couldn't inspect the website repo from this sandbox.)

Guild ID for the Discord jump link: the 5 resource channels all live in one guild — can I hard-code that guild ID in shared config, or should it be stored per-channel (e.g. read from `discord_channels.guild_id` if that table exists on the shared project)?
Where should SQL health-check snippets live — a new `banodoco-website/docs/sql/` or directly in `supabase/` at the workspace root?
`system_logs` insert shape: the brief mentions 'existing infrastructure' but I couldn't inspect it from this sandbox — what columns does it require (component, level, payload JSON, message, etc.)?

success_criteria.criterion: All migrations in Phase 1 apply cleanly via `supabase db reset` on a scratch branch with no errors

`assets` table gains all 7 new columns (source, discord_thread_id, discord_channel_id, imported_at, last_synced_at, reactions_reached_threshold_at, is_hidden) and a unique partial index on `discord_thread_id`
`asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media`, `media_import_jobs`, and `discord_resource_sync_cursors` tables exist with the columns, constraints, and indexes described in Steps 3–4

RLS is enabled on `asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media`, `media_import_jobs`, and `discord_resource_sync_cursors`; `asset_comments`/`asset_comment_media` have public-read SELECT policies; the latter two have no policies (service-role only)
Promoter edge function with `DRY_RUN=true` logs the set of assets/comments/media-jobs it would write and performs zero DB mutations

A live run of the promoter produces `assets` rows with `source='discord_import'`, `admin_status='Listed'`, correct `type` per channel, populated `discord_thread_id`/`discord_channel_id`, and corresponding `asset_comments` rows for replies
Running the promoter twice in a row produces zero net new `assets`, `asset_comments`, or `media_import_jobs` rows (full idempotency)

Media importer processes a single seeded `media_import_jobs` row end-to-end: creates a `media` row with the correct classification + populated Cloudflare/Storage URL, creates the matching `asset_media` or `asset_comment_media` junction row, and sets job `status='done'` with `media_id`
Rehosted video URLs return a valid HLS playlist (HTTP 200 with `#EXTM3U` in body); rehosted image URLs return HTTP 200 with a supported image content-type

A failed job (simulated by pointing at a deleted Discord message) increments `attempts`, records a `last_error`, and is re-queued with a growing `locked_until`; after 5 attempts it moves to `status='failed'` and stops retrying
pg_cron has two registered schedules (`discord-resource-promoter` every 10 min, `discord-media-importer` every 2 min) both invoking the correct edge-function URLs with service-role auth

After a full backfill cycle, qualifying OPs from all 5 channels (thread_id == message_id, reaction_count >= 5, within 60-day window) are represented in `assets` with `source='discord_import'`, and no OPs below the threshold are imported
A Discord message edit after import updates `asset_comments.content` and `discord_edited_at` on the next promoter run; a Discord message deletion sets `asset_comments.is_deleted=true`
ResourceModal shows the new 'Made with this' grid and 'Discussion' thread only for assets where `source='discord_import'`; existing manual assets render unchanged

Each rendered comment shows author avatar + name from `members`, markdown-rendered content, reply chip when applicable, inline attachment thumbnails with a working lightbox, reaction count, and a 'View on Discord' link that opens the correct message
`useAssetComments` fetches the joined data in a single Supabase query and returns comments ordered by `discord_created_at` ascending, excluding deleted rows
Resource cards show a subtle 'From Discord' badge with the channel name for imported assets
`assets.is_hidden=true` suppresses the asset from the website listing and modal
Existing website tests and Supabase type generation still pass after schema additions
Promoter wall-clock stays under 30s per invocation; importer stays under 400s — observed in edge-function logs across several runs
Health-check SQL snippets return usable output (stuck jobs, 24h failures, cursor staleness) against the live tables
No Cloudflare Stream quota / storage cost surprises during 60-day backfill — monitored via Cloudflare + Supabase dashboards after first full cycle
Per-phase rollback documentation exists and accurately reflects the migrations, cron jobs, and edge functions introduced

assumptions: Members table on the shared Supabase project is named `members` (confirmed from `brain-of-bndc/src/common/storage_handler.py:190-209`); all new FKs target `members(member_id)`. If the website already uses a `discord_members` alias, add a view rather than renaming.

Discord `attachment.id` is derived at import time by parsing the CDN URL path (`/attachments/{channel_id}/{attachment_id}/{filename}`), since the archive pipeline preserves only `{url, filename}` per `scripts/archive_discord.py:463-469`. Fallback dedup: `(discord_message_id, filename)` when parsing fails.

OP attachments use the website's existing manual-upload classification value (likely `'post'` or similar) rather than a new enum value; only reply attachments use the new `'discord-comment'` classification per the brief.
`admin_status='Listed'` and asset `type` values `'lora'` and `'tool'` are already valid in the existing `assets` CHECK constraints / enums on the website.

Migration files live in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` with the standard `<timestamp>_<slug>.sql` naming; Edge Functions live in `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/functions/` with the existing `_shared/` directory.
pg_cron invokes edge functions via `net.http_post` with the service-role key read from `app.settings.service_role_key` (a Supabase-vault-backed setting) — no literal key in migrations.

The `refresh-media-urls` edge function (documented in `brain-of-bndc/structure.md:196-226`) is available on the shared Supabase project and can be called by `discord-media-importer` to refresh expired Discord CDN signatures; Discord CDN URLs with a valid `?ex=` timestamp are reused as-is.
All 5 resource channels share a single guild ID, which will be hard-coded as a shared constant for building 'View on Discord' jump links.

Cloudflare Stream (tus upload) is the existing destination for video assets on the website; Supabase Storage `user-uploads` bucket handles images and other file types. Discord imports use path prefix `user-uploads/discord-imports/{discord_message_id}/{attachment_id}-{filename}`.
`system_logs` accepts at minimum `{component TEXT, level TEXT, message TEXT, payload JSONB}` — both edge functions log failures there; exact shape verified when implementing.

Discord messages' `reaction_count` column on `discord_messages` is maintained by the existing archive (any emoji counts toward the total per `brain-of-bndc/src/common/db_handler.py:783-828`), so the `>= 5` threshold is evaluated directly without joining `discord_reactions`.

The banodoco-website `ResourceModal.tsx` uses a single `Asset` record from Supabase and has an existing gallery strip for primary media + `asset_media`; the new sections slot in below that gallery and render only when `asset.source === 'discord_import'`.
A markdown renderer (likely `react-markdown` or equivalent) is already present in the website; if not, adding it is an in-scope dependency addition.

Storage cost of a 60-day Discord backfill across 5 forum channels with ~15 qualifying OPs / 30 days is under the Cloudflare Stream free-tier + Supabase Storage allowance thresholds currently in use — monitored rather than enforced up front.

Archive pipeline in brain-of-bndc is out of scope; the promoter reads `discord_messages` / `discord_reactions` / `members` as they exist today and upserts a thin `members` row only when FK validation would otherwise fail.
